highlights.summary: The Allnighters dropped a tough one to the Paddy Kelleys 11-5 on Wednesday night at Marrs Park, which did not live up to the hype it should have after the Nighties crushed them in the playoffs last Fall, but the story of the night instead had nothing to do with what happened on the field. Before the first pitch was even thrown, Coach Baz forgot the scorebook, Dermbo McDermott decided to grab a taco while the rest of the team stood waiting on the diamond to start the game, and Farrell had to leave early after his allergies put him in cardiac arrest. Mike (The Stoic) Stoico somehow managed to break his finger on what should have been a routine fly ball - which he dropped of course. With all that chaos it is a miracle the Nighters even scored 5. The Allnighters were also missing their big gun Brendan (BTro) Trohon who chose love over ball this day, who would have provided some much needed pop in the lineup. On the bright side Anthony (Ant) Gagliano and Mike (The OG) Russo both went 2-for-2 at the plate, making everyone question why these two are not hitting at the top of the order - especially when two other Nighters struck out, which as any ball player knows, is basically unforgivable in slow pitch. Coach made a highlight reel catch on a fly ball that required him to run what felt like a mile, which for him running to fridge is hard enough. And through it all, Russo was absolutely the reason the Allnighters stayed in the game, holding the Kelleys to 5 earned runs over 7 innings despite the defensive miscues behind him that inflated the damage to 11. The Kelleys may have doubled their payroll but the Nighters are not worried. Everyone needs to hit the cages this week and the Allnighters will be back in first place where they belong. Go Nighters!

Allnighters 'walked' passed the Wolfpack 11-4 at Marrs Park on Monday in a dominant home performance. The 'Nighties jumped out early with two runs in the first inning aided by excessive Wolfpack walks, and never looked back. Joe Z led the offense going 3-for-4 with a double and driving in two, while Eric (E) Moody went 2-for-2. Darren (Dermbo) McDermott, Jason Farrell, Coach Baz, and Mike Russo each chipped in with key hits. And even rookie Ace'ssad got a hit...litterly from the bench.  P, who's shed a couple of 30 packs from the waistline coming into the season could have used some bud light power but instead popped out to the 'short fielder'.  A positive sign on the night was very few runners left on base, thankfully due to Darren removing the lead in his shoes, the intimidation factor or our own State P Stoico, our own McDs terrorist Thomas, and of course the 'one man toking machine SCS', while most of the outs were hard-hit balls. Key highlight was Ant' going for it all into the plate but was robbed due to the blue blind mice umping as usual. Ryan came in to hold down 1st to give P some needed rest. Mike (The OG) Russo was dominant on the mound again, going all 7 innings allowing just 4 runs on 10 hits. Even a long bomb by the Wolfpack in the 7th did not rattle Russo or the Nighters. The defense, especailly the golden outfield crew, made some clutch catches in the middle innings to truly deliver a heart punch to the Pack. Next up - the newly stacked Paddy Kelleys who appear to have doubled their payroll in one last push for glory. The Nighters will need to bring the juice!

Allnighters could not hold onto the lead against Pocket 9s on Wednesday at Raddin Park and fell 5-4 despite leading by three going into the bottom of the 5th. Robert (The Godfather) Barrows was the offensive highlight for AllNighters going 3 for 3 with three line shots to the right side, while Patrick Hosman, Brendan (Btro) Trohon, and Ryan Overberg each added two hits. A single by Overberg put AllNighters on the board in the top of the second and then the crew rallied in the 4th. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Unfortunately they left 11 men on base, hit into two double plays, and stranded the bases loaded in the top of the 7th. Barrows, Overberg, and Joe Zametjis each drove in one run. Pocket 9s finished things off with a walk-off single RBI in the bottom of the 7th. If the Allnighters look to repeat this year, they better get their bats a little warmer.

Allnighters defeated Franchise 9-5 on Monday. The 'Nighties jumped out to the lead in the bottom of the first inning after Brendan (Btro) Trohon singled scoring one run, Joe Zametjis homered scoring three runs, and Darren (Dermbo) McDermott singled scoring one run. A single by Brendan (Bmac) McCarthy, a single by Trohon, and a double by Jason Farrell helped AllNighters extend their early lead in the second. Mike (The OG) Russo earned the win for AllNighters. The starting pitcher gave up seven hits and five runs over seven innings striking out none and walking none. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Patrick Hosman, McCarthy, Eric (E) Moody, and Trohon each collected two hits. Zametjis led AllNighters with three runs batted in going 1-for-3 on the day.
